metadata.title:
BOTH SIDES SET


OUT ARGUMENTS

President of School Board and the Tax.
payers League Discuss the


Bonding Project.

pages.raw_text:
Interest in the comma Jond cleo
ton for school improvements is be-
coming intense, and the outcome of
the project at the polls will settle a
fight which, while quietly conducted
is devoid of none of the elements of
strenuous contest. In order that
readers of The Press may more fully
understand the contentious of the op-
posing factions, the following COM
parison of arguments has been Sc
cured:


In a statement issued by a COM
mittee of the Taxpayers league, con-
sisting of w. s. Norman. E. H. jamie
son and George S. Brooke, the follow
ing 10 reasons for not bonding the
school district for 100000 are given


l. Because the average seating ca
pacity per school room in the city
during the past year was As, whereas
the average daily attendance was
but So.


2. Because a comparison for sew
eral years past shows the average in
crease in attendance has been about
10 per cent, which would make the
average daily attendance for the cur
rent year about 1000 and the seating
capacity S243.


3. Because the average number be-
longing in. one of the heaviest months
of the past year was s7Ss, against a
seating capacity Of S24S.


I. Because, although some Of the
rooms may be overcrowded, rear
rangement of the attendance would
overcome this difficulty.


8. Because If the bond issue were
to go through. the additions to be
built would give a seating capacity of
1888 against an estimated enroll
ment of 1900.


s. Because the committee regards
1t as preposterous to bond the district
for an additional $500, 900 under these
conditions.


T. Because the cost of building
at the present time is at it's very
highest as compared with former
years.


9. Because the estimated cost of
contemplated new buildings is mug
more than the cost of new business
blocks recently completed. whereas 1t
should be much less.


.. Because the present high school
is the pride of Spokane and should
not be succeeded by another building
less advantageously situated.


10. Because the floating indebted
ness Of the district Is already $751;
4sl.7s.
